result:
**`pink-cybertruck-web-level-4.html` is done, and level-3 is untouched** (its SHA-256 is unchanged: `422df4c5…`). I verified level-4 in a real browser: three.js loads, the canvas renders, zero console errors, and the dot wave animates in both themes.

The component you gave me is React + Tailwind + `next-themes`, and this page is a single self-contained HTML file, so I ported it rather than dropped it in. What carried over verbatim: the 40 × 60 grid at 150-unit separation, the `(0, 355, 1220)` camera, the two-sine wave, fog, and the 8px attenuated points.

Three things I changed deliberately:

**It's a real background, not a section.** The original is `fixed inset-0 -z-1` — a full-viewport layer, not a block in the flow. I kept that, which meant the page's base color had to move from `body` to `html` and the sections had to become translucent (new `--veil` / `--veil-deep` tokens per theme), otherwise the opaque sections would have covered the canvas completely and you'd never see it. The footer takes the least veil, so — matching "at the bottom of the page" — the wave reads strongest down there while staying a faint texture behind the hero and mid-page. Cards, specs, and the receipt keep solid backgrounds so text contrast is unaffected.

**It re-themes with the page.** `next-themes` isn't here, so the dot color and fog come from `--dot` / `--dot-a` tokens and a `MutationObserver` on `data-theme` repaints them when you hit the toggle. (Side note: the original pushed `200, 200, 200` into a color buffer that three.js reads as 0–1, so those dots were silently clamped to pure white. I used proper 0–1 values.)

**I used the classic three.js build (r159), not the ESM one.** These demo files get opened straight off disk, and module scripts are blocked at a `file://` origin — the ESM version would have left the page with no surface at all when double-clicked.

Reduced-motion users get one rendered frame and no animation loop, and the loop pauses when the tab is hidden.
